questions.question: Why is logical acquisition often the first method attempted by an examiner?
expectedPoints: It is the least invasive method.
It is fast and simple to perform.
It requires no special access or device modification.
It provides a good overview of active user data (contacts, messages, calls).

questions.question: Compare and contrast Physical and Logical acquisition methods. Discuss the types of data recovered, invasiveness, and typical use cases for each.
expectedPoints: Physical: Creates a bit-for-bit image of storage; recovers deleted data/unallocated space; more invasive/complex; used for full analysis when possible.
Logical: Extracts logical files via OS; only recovers active data; non-invasive/quick; used for initial data triage or when physical is blocked.
Contrast: Data depth (physical = deep, logical = surface), invasiveness, tool requirements, and speed.
Use Case: Physical for serious investigations needing deleted data; Logical for quick preview or evidence confirmation.
